title: status-pipe launch file
description: Committed at .status-pipe/launch.json: how to launch this repo's agents. A launch entry has three orthogonal fields. `id` is the role / lookup key: two ids are reserved — `tick` (the planner) and `worker` (the dispatch template the supervisor instantiates once per item the planner writes to orchestrator.json.dispatch, resolving %prompt% (the worker's `claude -p` argument) and %worktree% (its cwd)); any other id is a generic supervised agent. `type` is the work mechanism: `claude` (the CLI with default prompt/args supplied — only command/args you want to override), `exec` (an explicit command/args), or `built-in` (the in-process deterministic planner — no external process, valid only on the reserved `tick` id). `lifetime` is how a single supervised process is managed: `scheduled` (run, exit, relaunch intervalMinutes later) or `daemon` (long-running, restarted on death); the `worker` template ignores it (on-demand by role). The token %home% in command/args/cwd/env values expands to the user's home directory at spawn time (deliberately not shell-style ${...}). Execution is trust-gated: workspace trust plus per-content-hash approval of the complete RESOLVED entry (id, type, command, args, stdin, cwd, env, lifetime). Never launched from a linked worktree.

allOf.$comment: exec (the default when type is absent) needs an explicit command; claude and built-in supply (or carry no) command.
Only the reserved roles (tick/worker) carry built-in default args; a generic claude entry must supply its own NON-EMPTY args, or the supervisor cannot resolve a command and drops it.
built-in is the in-process planner: valid only on the reserved tick id.

id.description: Role / lookup key, unique within the file; supervisor state is keyed (repo, id) and the id is part of the trust hash. Reserved: `tick` (planner) and `worker` (dispatch template). When omitted, readers synthesize a positional agent-<index> — set it explicitly when the file lists more than one agent: reordering re-keys supervisor state, and any id change invalidates the entry's trust approval.

type.description: Work mechanism. claude: command defaults to `claude` and args default to the canonical invocation for the reserved role (worker: `-p %prompt%` + stream-json/verbose/auto flags; tick: `-p /status-pipe:tick --max-concurrent 3`); override args to customize. exec: explicit command + args. built-in: the in-process deterministic planner (no external process, no command/args) — valid only on the reserved `tick` id. Absent ⇒ exec (legacy untyped entries carried a command).

stdin.description: Written to the child's stdin then closed; supports backends that take their prompt on stdin

cwd.description: Relative to the repo root for a scheduled/daemon entry. For the worker template it resolves against the dispatched %worktree% — a relative or empty cwd lands inside the worktree, not the repo root.

env.description: Merged over the inherited environment. Values support %home% (the user's home directory).

lifetime.description: Supervision style of a single supervised process. scheduled: one pass then exit, relaunched intervalMinutes after exit. daemon: long-running, restarted on death. Ignored for the worker template (on-demand by role). Absent ⇒ scheduled.

intervalMinutes.description: scheduled lifetime: minutes of quiet between passes, measured from exit (never overlapping)

timeoutMinutes.description: scheduled lifetime: hard wall-clock cap per pass; a pass still running after this is killed and recorded as a failure. Also the per-process cap for each worker the template spawns.
